mnemonics.title: P-R-I-V-A-T-E Recall
mnemonics.content: P-Private in implementation | R-Reuse common code | I-Internal only | V-Visible to interface only | A-Abstract not allowed | T-True helper methods | E-Encapsulation enhanced

acronyms.usage: The system that introduced private interface methods in Java 9 along with modularization features.

visualTips.concept: Private vs Default vs Static methods in interfaces
visualTips.visualization: Draw a hierarchy: Public abstract at top (contract), Default below (inherited but overridable), Private at bottom (hidden helper). Show arrows from Default methods pointing to Private methods as internal calls.
Before: Two default methods with identical 5-line code blocks - draw a box around the duplicate and show an arrow to a new private method that both call.
Triangle with public at top, default/protected in middle, private at bottom - shows decreasing accessibility scope for interface members.

analogies.concept: Private interface methods as kitchen staff
analogies.analogy: Think of an interface as a restaurant menu (public API). The private methods are like the kitchen staff - they do the actual work but customers (implementing classes) never see them. They just receive the final dish (default behavior).

analogies.concept: Private methods as private notes in a textbook
analogies.analogy: Public default methods are like chapter headings students see. Private methods are like the author's private notes in the margin - they're useful for organizing the content but aren't part of what students directly read.
